mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as long as rate trajectory is downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.08% as of April 2008 reflects Fed easing cycle begun in September 2007
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline. The mechanism activates when unemployment rises above a critical threshold indicating cyclical weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in April 2008, near cycle lows but beginning to inflect upward
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 683,974 million as of March 2008, showing early signs of demand weakness
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during RSAFS decline periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production decline combined with rising unemployment indicates supply-side contraction and labor market stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism requires joint deterioration in both production and employment to activate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% in 3 months AND PAYEMS growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 in March 2008, PAYEMS at 153,308k in April 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,200k units signals residential investment collapse and negative wealth effects on consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism activates when housing construction falls below critical threshold indicating demand shock.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,439k in March 2008, elevated but vulnerable to further decline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 1,200k, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rate indicates monetary tightness that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days. The mechanism requires both high inflation and high rates to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.06 in April 2008, FEDFUNDS at 3.08%
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression, or SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of jointly triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment decline signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer income, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when monthly payroll growth turns negative, indicating cyclical employment loss.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153,308k in April 2008, near peak but vulnerable to reversal
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following negative PAYEMS months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth combined with declining retail sales indicates demand composition shift away from consumption toward investment or net exports, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as consumer-dependent equities face headwinds. The mechanism requires simultaneous GDP strength and retail weakness to activate.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2% AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20,021.7B in Q4 2007, RSAFS at 683,974M in March 2008
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between GDPC1 and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.15), or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of jointly triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
